title: rubus apiculatus var. newbouldii
intro: Rubus apiculatus var. newbouldii, also known as Newbould's blackberry, is a species of thorny shrub in the rose family. It is native to the Himalayas and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and as a natural antiseptic. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and tannins, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Newbould's blackberry is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
